steps.title: Identify the decisive clue
steps.content: ”Every conceivable obstacle” tells us the author means obstacles that can be conceived of or imagined. The word “every” signals she's describing the full range of obstacles.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the answer
steps.content: ”Conceivable” means “able to be thought of” or “imaginable”—which equals “possible.” D captures this directly. A, B, and C don't express this sense of “imaginable” or “possible”. That matches choice D — D is correct.
